sentiment: Mixed - Positive on product, frustrated with process
summary: Mike called requesting credit line increase from $185K to $250K. Fleet expanding with 8 new trucks arriving in April. He mentioned a competitor (WEX) is offering lower transaction fees and faster line increase approvals. Frustrated that our line increase process takes 5-7 business days. However, expressed strong satisfaction with Coast's fuel discount network and real-time transaction visibility. Asked about volume discount tiers for 50+ truck fleets.
action_items: Process line increase request - pull 90-day financials
Competitive analysis on WEX fee structure
Check eligibility for enterprise tier upgrade

summary: Routine quarterly check-in. Mike reports 3 new drivers added last month, all cards activated smoothly. No current complaints. Asked about Coast's EV fleet card support for future planning - they're evaluating electric trucks for urban routes. Mike mentioned they're bidding on a USPS last-mile contract that could double their fleet size to ~100 trucks within 12 months.
action_items: Flag for enterprise team if USPS contract wins
Share EV fleet card roadmap info

summary: Dispute on two transactions totaling $847.32. Driver used card at non-network station (independent truck stop in rural Alabama). Mike didn't realize the station wasn't in network. Investigated and confirmed legitimate purchase. Waived the out-of-network surcharge as a goodwill gesture. Mike appreciated the quick resolution and thanked the team.
action_items: Goodwill credit applied - $42.50
Send Mike updated network station map for Alabama routes

summary: Called to follow up on account review and request for updated bank statements. Raj was evasive - claimed his accountant was on vacation and couldn't access QuickBooks. When pressed about the high utilization rate (70.7%), he said they had a 'big contract' but wouldn't provide details. Promised to send bank statements by end of week. Asked again about increasing credit line. Seemed nervous when asked about the 6 new card requests exceeding their stated fleet size of 12.
action_items: Escalate to risk team if docs not received by Friday 3/14
Cross-reference fleet size with FMCSA records
Monitor transactions daily

summary: Onboarding follow-up call. Raj immediately asked about increasing credit line from $45K to $100K - only 3 weeks into the account. Explained our standard 90-day review process for new accounts. He pushed back hard, claiming he needed it for a 'seasonal surge.' When asked about seasonal patterns, answers were vague. Reluctantly accepted the 90-day timeline.
action_items: Note: early line increase request is a risk signal
Standard 90-day review scheduled for April 22

summary: Quarterly Business Review. Linda is very happy with Coast - described us as 'the best vendor relationship we have.' Confirming annual contract renewal. Key discussion points: (1) API integration for their fleet management system - their engineering team wants transaction data feeds. (2) Acquisition of Midwest Carriers (~30 trucks) - want to onboard them under Greenfield's account with consolidated billing. (3) Interested in volume discount tiers. (4) Asked about Coast Rewards program. Linda mentioned their board is pushing for 15% cost reduction across all vendors next FY - wants us to help build a business case showing Coast ROI.
action_items: Send API documentation to Linda's engineering team
Schedule Midwest Carriers onboarding call
Prepare ROI analysis for board presentation
Connect with product team on volume discount proposal

summary: Linda called about the Midwest Carriers acquisition. Deal is expected to close April 15. They want to onboard ~30 trucks immediately after close. Discussed consolidated billing options and whether Midwest's existing fuel card data can be migrated. Linda wants a dedicated onboarding plan. Also asked about Coast's data export capabilities for their annual audit.
action_items: Prepare Midwest Carriers onboarding timeline
Check data migration feasibility
Send data export documentation for audit team

summary: Dave called to request 7 additional cards for new HVAC technicians they're hiring. Very happy with BuildOps integration - says it's saving them 10+ hours per month on job costing. Asked if Coast cards can be set up with per-job spending limits that sync with BuildOps job IDs. Also mentioned they're opening a second location in Savannah, GA and will need cards for that crew too.
action_items: Process 7 new card requests
Check BuildOps job-level spending limit feasibility with product
Flag Savannah expansion for future card order

summary: Called Carlos about low card activation (only 3 of 10 cards used). He said his crew is 'still getting used to it' and that some installers prefer using personal cards and expensing later. Promised to push the team to switch but didn't seem very engaged. Call was short - Carlos said he was on a job site. Suggested a quick training session for his team but he said 'maybe next week.'
action_items: Schedule team training call for week of 3/17
Send activation guide to Carlos via email
Consider small activation incentive
